services.service_name: Provision of Technical Assistance on Crops, Livestock and Fisheries
services.description: Technical assistance on crops, livestock and fisheries in the form of services, materials, or inputs provided to farmers.

requirements.document: Sample or picture of damaged crop/livestock/fisheries

process_flow.client_action: Sign in the logbook, request for appropriate technical assistance, submit to interview and provide information requested by the Agriculturist.

agency_actions.action: Get the client's information and conduct interview.
Analyze the problem and recommend solution or give IEC materials as reference.
If immediate action is needed, recommend an on-site visit and provide schedule of visit.

process_flow.client_action: Take note of the schedule of farm visit and assist the Agriculturist during the on-site visitation. Take note of the diagnostic and advice.

agency_actions.action: Conduct farm visit.

process_flow.client_action: Implement recommended action.

agency_actions.action: Provide written diagnostic and recommendation.

faqs.question: Who can avail of the technical assistance on crops, livestock, and fisheries?
faqs.answer: This service is available to farmers in the municipality.

faqs.question: What should I bring when requesting technical assistance?
faqs.answer: Bring a sample or picture of the damaged crop, livestock, or fisheries, and a report of incidence.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for technical assistance?
faqs.answer: No, technical assistance on crops, livestock, and fisheries is provided free of charge.

faqs.question: Will the agriculturist visit my farm?
faqs.answer: Yes, if immediate action is needed, an on-site farm visit will be scheduled. The farm visit may take 4 hours to 1 full day.

faqs.question: Where do I go to request technical assistance?
faqs.answer: Proceed to the Municipal Agriculture Office, sign in the logbook, and request for the appropriate technical assistance.

services.description: A service assistance to farmers raising a female carabao (caraheifer) aged 3 years and above.

process_flow.client_action: Wait for announcement.

agency_actions.action: Announce the date of palpitation and estrous determination of carabao through meetings, letters, CATV, or Bandillo.

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the site on the date for the determination if carabao is in heat or ready for insemination or breeding.

agency_actions.action: Determine if carabao is in heat (natural or for estrous synchronization).
If in natural heat, perform artificial insemination.
If to be estrous synchronized, inject hormone.
Record the animal's condition in the estrous synchronization form.
agency_actions.person_responsible: Livestock Technician, OPV / Agricultural Technician, MAO

process_flow.client_action: If carabao is inseminated, return the animal to the farm.

agency_actions.action: Advise farmer to take care of the carabao.

process_flow.client_action: If carabao is injected with hormone, follow the advice of the technician on the given schedule of follow-up AI and the proper care of the carabao.

agency_actions.action: Advise farmer to return after 3 days for artificial insemination.
Determine if the carabao is ready for AI. Conduct AI.

process_flow.client_action: Return on the schedule for final AI.

agency_actions.action: Advise farmer to return to the site the following day for follow-up AI.
Consolidation of data and terminal report for the entire activity.

faqs.question: Who can avail of the artificial insemination service for carabao?
faqs.answer: Owners of a female carabao (caraheifer) aged 3 years and above can avail of this service.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for artificial insemination services?
faqs.answer: No, the artificial insemination service for carabao is free of charge.

faqs.question: How will I know when the artificial insemination schedule is?
faqs.answer: The Municipal Agriculture Office will announce the date through community meetings, letters, CATV, or Bandillo.

faqs.question: What happens if my carabao is not yet in heat?
faqs.answer: The livestock technician will inject a hormone for estrous synchronization and advise you to return after 3 days for the artificial insemination.

faqs.question: How many visits are needed for the entire AI process?
faqs.answer: It may take multiple visits depending on the carabao's condition — initial determination, follow-up after 3 days if hormone was given, and a final follow-up AI the next day.

services.service_name: Provision of Technical Assistance for the Availment of Vegetable Seeds
services.description: An assistance in the form of vegetable seeds provided to farmers and individuals who engage in crop production and backyard farming.

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Agriculture Office and inquire about the availability of vegetable seeds.

agency_actions.action: Conduct interview regarding the size and location of the area.

process_flow.client_action: Receive the vegetable seeds.

agency_actions.action: Release available seeds.
Record in the logbook.

faqs.question: Who can avail of vegetable seeds from the Municipal Agriculture Office?
faqs.answer: Farmers and individuals engaged in crop production and backyard farming can avail of this service.

faqs.question: What do I need to bring when requesting vegetable seeds?
faqs.answer: Bring a picture of your area and information about the size of the area where you plan to plant.

faqs.question: Is there a cost for the vegetable seeds?
faqs.answer: No, the vegetable seeds are provided free of charge.

faqs.question: How long does it take to receive vegetable seeds?
faqs.answer: The process takes approximately 20 minutes from interview to release of seeds, subject to availability.

services.service_name: Provision of Technical Assistance for the Availment of Fingerlings
services.description: Assistance provided to fisher folk including fingerlings, management, and technical advisory.

process_flow.client_action: Wait for announcement.

agency_actions.action: Announce the date of availability of fingerlings through meetings, CATV, or Bandillo.

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Agriculture Office and inquire about the program.

agency_actions.action: Conduct interview, get the information on area and location of fishpond, and include the client name in the list of farmers who will avail fingerlings.

process_flow.client_action: Return to the Office of Municipal Agriculture on the specified date. Receive the fingerlings and sign in the recipients logbook.

agency_actions.action: Release fingerlings and record in the logbook.
Provide monthly report for the status of fingerlings.

faqs.question: Who can avail of fingerlings from the Municipal Agriculture Office?
faqs.answer: Farmers and fisher folk with fishponds can avail of this service.

faqs.question: What information do I need to provide to avail fingerlings?
faqs.answer: You need to provide the size and location of your fishpond.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for the fingerlings?
faqs.answer: No, fingerlings are provided free of charge.

faqs.question: How will I know when fingerlings are available?
faqs.answer: The Municipal Agriculture Office will announce the availability date through community meetings, CATV, or Bandillo.

faqs.question: Do I need to return to the office to receive the fingerlings?
faqs.answer: Yes, you need to register first during your initial visit, then return on the specified date to receive the fingerlings and sign in the recipients logbook.

services.description: Services provided to pet owners where dogs and cats are subjected to anti-rabies vaccination.

process_flow.client_action: Wait for announcement.

agency_actions.action: Announce the date of vaccination through meetings, CATV, or Bandillo.

process_flow.client_action: Bring the animal to the site of vaccination together with the animal record if available. Approach the vaccinator or Agricultural Technologist for interview and fill out the animal information form.

agency_actions.action: Advise dog/cat owner to fill out animal information form.
Conduct interview.

process_flow.client_action: Assist the vaccinator in holding the animal for vaccination.

agency_actions.action: Vaccinate the animal.
agency_actions.person_responsible: Livestock Technician, OPV / Agricultural Technologist, MAO

agency_actions.action: Give advice on how to take care of newly vaccinated animal.
agency_actions.person_responsible: Livestock Technician, OPV / Agricultural Technologist, MAO

agency_actions.action: Provide terminal report as to the number of animals that underwent vaccination.

faqs.question: Who can avail of the anti-rabies vaccination service?
faqs.answer: Dog and cat owners in the municipality can avail of this service.

faqs.question: What do I need to bring for the anti-rabies vaccination?
faqs.answer: Bring your pet and its animal record if available. You will also be asked to fill out an animal information form on-site.

faqs.question: Is there a fee for anti-rabies vaccination?
faqs.answer: No, anti-rabies vaccination is provided free of charge.

faqs.question: How will I know when the vaccination schedule is?
faqs.answer: The Municipal Agriculture Office will announce the date through community meetings, CATV, or Bandillo.

faqs.question: What should I do after my pet is vaccinated?
faqs.answer: The livestock technician or agricultural technologist will advise you on how to properly care for your newly vaccinated animal.

services.service_name: Provision of Technical Assistance for the Availment of Certified Seeds or Hybrid Palay Seeds
services.description: Assistance provided to rice farmers who want to avail any of the certified and hybrid palay seeds.

requirements.document: Size of the area and personal information of farmer/owner/tenant

process_flow.client_action: Wait for announcement.

agency_actions.action: Announce the date of availability, variety, and prices of seeds through meetings, CATV, or Bandillo.

process_flow.client_action: Proceed to the Municipal Agriculture Office and inquire about the program. Submit to interview.

agency_actions.action: Conduct interview, get information regarding the rice field, and include the client name in the list of farmers who will avail of the palay seeds.
Inform the farmers on the date of availability of certified seeds or hybrid seeds.

process_flow.client_action: Return to the Municipal Agriculture Office and pay for the seeds. Then get the palay seeds.

agency_actions.action: Release the palay seeds. Accept and record payment.
agency_actions.fees: Inbred seeds: None. Hybrid seeds: PHP 950.00 per 3-kilo bag

process_flow.client_action: Sign in the recipient logbook.

agency_actions.action: Issue acknowledgement receipt.

services.total_fees: Inbred seeds: None. Hybrid seeds: PHP 950.00 per 3-kilo bag

faqs.question: Who can avail of certified or hybrid palay seeds?
faqs.answer: Rice farmers — whether owner or tenant — can avail of this service.

faqs.question: Are the palay seeds free?
faqs.answer: Inbred seeds are free of charge. Hybrid seeds cost PHP 950.00 per 3-kilo bag.

faqs.question: What information do I need to provide to avail of palay seeds?
faqs.answer: You need to provide the size of your rice field and your personal information as farmer, owner, or tenant.

faqs.question: How will I know when the palay seeds are available?
faqs.answer: The Municipal Agriculture Office will announce the availability date, variety, and prices through community meetings, CATV, or Bandillo.

faqs.question: Will I receive an official receipt after purchasing hybrid seeds?
faqs.answer: Yes, an acknowledgement receipt will be issued after payment and release of seeds.
